raw_input:
[2.0/5] GLO Science Teeth Whitening Pen I am not a fan of this GLO Science Teeth Whitening Pen. The clicking mechanism is super annoying- a twisting mechanism would have been so much easier. The thick, bulky, silicone tipped applicator is annoying as well and a lot of the gel gets left in its little bumps and grooves. It doesn't apply smoothly or evenly at all. I have been using it twice per day but have not seen any results. The taste of the gel is absolutely horrible- there's like a weird floral perfume quality to the taste. Do not like. [3.0/5] Good so far Love the convenience of this pen, just wish the gel would come out a better way. First off, it took FIVEver to expression the gel out. I think I clicked the bottom 50 times before a tiny bead of gel started to appear, I had to aggressively click and slam the pen onto the counter to get more product out. Once I was finally able to get enough product out, I dried my teeth (after flossing and brushing first) and wiped the silicone bristles onto my teeth from premolar to premolar on my upper and lower teeth, I used the box it came in to fan and dry off my teeth. I did this before bed time so I wouldn’t have the urge to eat or drink after. The gel will become stringy once your inner lips and cheek touch the gel and there’s a mild taste to it. So far I’ve used this pen nightly for 4 days and I’ve seen a tiny bit of results. You’re supposed to use it twice a day for 2 weeks. I haven’t experienced any sensitivity from the gel, partly because I’ll use sensodyne toothpaste to brush and the gel is formulated with potassium nitrate that helps prevent sensitivity. Other good ingredients include xylitol and natural antioxidants. Overall good product, just need a better way to get the product out without having to click it. Twisting would be a better option and maybe transparent packaging so you know how much product is left. [3.0/5] Needs to be used twice a day for two weeks The pen is super convenient, easy to carry and conceal. You push the base to get the gel started, once you start to feel resistance, you know it's at the top. When using it, ensure your teeth are dry enough to hold the gel. I like that it is no rinse needed, just wait 20-30 minutes after applying to eat or drink. While the idea is great, the results were minimal. To see max results, the gel needs to be applied twice a day for two weeks. At that rate you would be better going a different route. [3.0/5] Hard to apply accurately After 3 applications I do not see the effects yet, although I feel the sensitivity, so it has the peroxid working.

My main complain is the applicator tip. The goo comes out in the middle of it, and it has some rubber bumpy thingies around. The surface of the tip is relatively large and you do not see the edges or the goo why applying it (as it faces your teeth). So I would say it is much harder to apply this right next to your gum line accurately, than some other whitening pens having a pointy bristle top.

The idea is good, but currently I would prefer a smaller tip (even as a 6ft tall male).

3 stars for now, let me use it for a while and update the review if my opinion changes. [3.0/5] easy for sensitive teeth GLO Science Teeth Whitening Pen 35 Aplications, Safe and Efective, No Sensitivity, Easy to Use Dentist Invented Whitening Treatment, Travel-Friendly

Are my teeth whiter? Yes. It's not so white that anyone will see me and be like 'wow your teeth are so white!' but it is whiter and I notice it, which I prefer. I have very sensitive teeth and almost no enamel. The pen is a click pen, and you have click quite a lot to get it started. I thought mine was faulty at first, and the clicker even got stuck half way down but I smacked it and it popped back and I kept clicking. The silicone tip has little bristles and it does a good job of coming off on your teeth - I was worried the gel wouldn't grip onto my teeth and would slide, but it didn't. It does have a cinnamon taste, and it does dry in 30 seconds, it was easy enough to smile for 30 seconds and it was on there. Truly I forgot about it after that (don't eat or drink for 30 min if you can). Are my teeth whiter? I couldn't tell after 1 use, but after two, I also couldn't tell... BUT I took a before and after photo (which I'll save you from because my lips look awful right now) and my teeth are definitely visibly whiter! I'm happy with this, it's easy to put on, doesn't make my teeth sensitive, works for whitening, dries quickly, and you don't even have to spit it out. [4.0/5] Easy application This product is currently on the trial period and so far I haven’t seen any improvement to my teeth after 2 weeks. I’m hoping it will help my teeth become whiter. The good side about the product is the neat packaging and how easy, simple, and practical way to use it. With a thin layer of the gel, brush it on the teeth, the taste is somewhat tolerable, then let it dry for 30 seconds and no more rinsing. [2.0/5] Horrible Flavor, Non-Existent Results Being a tea and coffee drinker, my teeth have a tendency to stain from time to time. I have used OTC whitening products in the past with a lot of success. I was originally looking at the Colgate pen, but saw very mixed reviews and decided to go with this one.

Packaging, top notch. The colors, construction, and branding are all on point. However, that's where, for me, the good news ends.

To start the pen, be prepared to click 30-40 times to get the first bit of gel to come out. After that, sometimes just opening the pen is enough to cause it to ooze... other times, you are going to be clicking 5-10 times to get it to dispense the gel.

To prepare the teeth, I use a Kleenex to wipe the teeth down so they are dry. I apply a generous coating of the gel to all the teeth in front (top and bottom)... Once applied, I leave the mouth open and teeth exposed to allow the coating to dry (suggested was 30 seconds... I went up to 1.5 minutes with no difference). Once you close your mouth, the product immediately starts foaming (H2O2). The flavor is not good... I know this is subjective... but it is like dropping cinnamon red hots into Vanilla Coke... letting it set until flat and then watering down to almost no flavor... [in short, back wash.]

After 9 days, there has been no change in the color of my teeth. I have used a color guide from my dentist to follow the process, and I have stayed right around a 6.

Horrible taste, ineffective whitening, more than $20... Yeah, not for me. [5.0/5] It works I have been using this for 4 days and the result is really there. Basically, I applied this composition to the fangs, as they differ in color from all other teeth.
In such a short period of time, I noticed a change of about 2 tones.
Despite the fact that the manufacturer writes that the composition is absolutely safe and natural, I would not use it for more than 7 days in a row. Although the instructions say about 2 weeks of use 2 times a day. So far, I have not risked for such a long period. It's still hydrogen peroxide.
But in general, this whitening pen really works and I cannot but rejoice it. [5.0/5] Great whitener! The glo to go teeth whitening pen is so easy to use! Whitening results each time I have used it! Easy to take anywhere. Great subtle flavor, not tingly or too strong. I love having this whitening pen on hand for when I feel like I need it! [1.0/5] Didn't work for me I used the pen a couple times and I am yet to see any results. The flavor is horrible. I thought it was expired initially because it tasted so gross. Also it took me a while to get the gel to come out. After clicking about 20 times it finally cam out. Not worth it. [4.0/5] Convenient and easy to use This whitening pen is convenient and easy to use!
The pen tip allows me to put the gel on parts of my teeth that whitening strips doesn't cover. After three consecutive days of use, my teeth were visibly and noticeably more white! [1.0/5] Pen doesn’t work The pen would not dispense the product. [5.0/5] Easy to use, visible results after third use. Very happy with this teeth-whitening product. This product works and is gentle, just be sure to follow the directions and don't overuse it.

My teeth are pretty white, but I'd noticed in the last year they were looking less bright. I drink coffee and coke, and I'm 45. This product appealed to me because it's very easy to use.

The pen has a clicker on the end to release the product and you apply it to the front of your teeth. I used it twice a day for the first three days and after achieving the results I wanted, I have been using at night every other day.

This product contains hydrogen peroxide for whitening, xylitol for oral health, and potassium nitrate to reduce sensitivity. When you apply the gel, you let it dry for 30 seconds, and they you can't drink or eat for 30 minutes. I would use this before bed, before working out, or after dinner while watching TV.

By the third application I could tell a big difference in the whiteness of my teeth. They were much brighter and whiter. I had just the slightest sensitivity after using this. Would recommend this product. Great value, easy-to-use and it works. [5.0/5] Quick results This teeth whitener is easy to use and my teeth looked whiter after just one use. I like that you only have to wait 20-30 minutes after applying before you can eat/drink and also that you don't have to brush your teeth afterward. When I first used I had to click the button probably 20 times before any of the gel dispensed but after that it was smooth and easy to apply. Right after I use this my teeth look noticeably whiter and brighter especially for the first day or two, but the brightness kind of fades although overall I am seeing, hopefully, a long lasting whiter smile. [4.0/5] Easy to use Sasy to use.. small and congenital [1.0/5] O Star since it was dried out and could not try!! It was dried out so I never got to even try it—now noo return

response:
Here is a summary of the reviews for the GLO Science Teeth Whitening Pen:

Overall, customer opinions are mixed, with results and ease of use being the main points of discussion.

On the positive side, several customers found the pen convenient, portable, and easy to use. Some users reported visible whitening results, with a few noticing improvements after just a few uses. The product contains beneficial ingredients like hydrogen peroxide, xylitol, and potassium nitrate, which some appreciated for its sensitivity-reducing properties. A handful of reviewers with sensitive teeth were pleased that the product did not cause discomfort.

On the negative side, a common complaint across many reviews was the difficulty dispensing the gel, often requiring 20 to 50 or more clicks before any product came out. The silicone tip applicator was frequently criticized for being bulky, making it hard to apply the gel evenly and accurately, especially near the gum line. Many reviewers disliked the taste, describing it as floral, chemical, or unpleasant. Several customers saw little to no whitening results even after consistent use over multiple days or weeks. One customer received a dried-out pen that was unusable.

In summary, the pen has potential for some users but is let down by a frustrating dispensing mechanism, an awkward applicator tip, an off-putting taste, and inconsistent whitening results.
